title: Daily Memo: Commercial Shipping and the Conflict in Iran
author: Geopolitical Futures
summary:
Tehran’s reprisals. Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said it would allow passage through the Strait of Hormuz to Arab or European countries that expel U.S. and Israeli ambassadors. A spokesperson said the IRGC would not allow the export of oil from the region to the U.S. or its partners “until further notice,” adding that Tehran […]

The post Daily Memo: Commercial Shipping and the Conflict in Iran appeared first on Geopolitical Futures.
full_text:
Tehran’s reprisals. Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said it would allow passage through the Strait of Hormuz to Arab or European countries that expel U.S. and Israeli ambassadors. A spokesperson said the IRGC would not allow the export of oil from the region to the U.S. or its partners “until further notice,” adding that Tehran […]

Geopolitical Futures (GPF) was founded in 2015 by George Friedman, international strategist and author of The Storm Before the Calm and The Next 100 Years. GPF is non-ideological, analyzes the world and forecasts the future using geopolitics: political, economic, military and geographic dimensions at the foundation of a nation.
